question:
Will Player H win the 2025 WTA Tokyo Women’s Singles Tournament?

Description: This polymarket will resolve to the player that wins the 2025 Toray Pan Pacific Open (WTA Tokyo) Women’s Singles Tournament.

The market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed player wins the 2025 WTA Tokyo Women’s Singles Tournament. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this player to win the tournament per the official rules of the WTA Tokyo tournament, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

evidences.title: 2025 Toray Pan Pacific Open (singles entrants excerpt)
evidences.content: Singles main-draw entrants (seeds) include Elena Rybakina, Ekaterina Alexandrova, Belinda Bencic, Linda Nosková, Diana Shnaider, Karolína Muchová, Victoria Mboko, and Sofia Kenin. Rankings are as of 13 October 2025. Other entrants listed as receiving wildcards into the singles draw include Bianca Andreescu, Nikola Bartůňková, Wakana Sonobe, and Moyuka Uchijima; one player is noted as entering using a protected ranking. The 2025 Toray Pan Pacific Open is a professional women's hard-court tournament and the 51st edition.

evidences.title: Madison Keys - Wikipedia (excerpt)
evidences.content: Excerpt notes career highlights and references covering her matches and tournament results. It also states that Keys was inspired to start playing tennis after watching Venus Williams at Wimbledon on TV, and that she turned professional on her 14th birthday.

evidences.title: 2025 Toray & Guangzhou Opens: Top Contenders & Predictions | RotoWire
evidences.content: hasn't prevented Bencic from posting winning head-to-head records against both Alexandrova (5-4) and Rybakina (2-1). [...] Women's main draw play at the 2025 Toray Pan Pacific Open and 2025 Guangzhou Open will begin Monday, Oct. 20. The Toray Open is a WTA 500 level event, while the Guangzhou Open is a WTA 250 event. These outdoor hard-court tournaments both have 32-player draws (28 for Toray with byes for the top four seeds) and feature numerous women's tennis stars, even after some last-minute withdrawals. There are still six top-20 players scheduled to take the court at the Toray Open, while the Guangzhou Open presents a nice opportunity for non-household names to take the spotlight, with the top seed clocking in at No. 40 in the rankings. Below are the top title contenders and dark horses for the 2025 Toray Pan Pacific Open and Guangzhou Open. ## Favorite to Win the 2025 Toray Open [...] Women's main draw play at the 2025 Toray Pan Pacific Open and 2025 Guangzhou Open will begin Monday, Oct. 20. The Toray Open is a WTA 500 level event, while the Guangzhou Open is a WTA 250 event. These outdoor hard-court tournaments both have 32-player draws (28 for Toray with byes for the t...

That's a really good return from Yastremska. Now, that's a wonderful pass as well. That's a really stylish way to finish things off. Schneider repels the threat of the fight back from Yastremska who was threatening to make things interesting with that hold to love and that break to love. But Schneider gets herself over the line in the opening set and she takes it by six games to three. Yeah, finishing with style, Dayana Yastremska. She got to love victory 6-1. A comfortable victory for the number seven seed. And Schneider, a semifinalist in Tokyo 12 months ago, is off and running in 2025. She's had some very impressive results on hard [...] in 2025. She's had some very impressive results on hard courts lately. That's another one. She wins it 6-3 6-1. Former player Daria Kasatkina. Nice simple finish. Oh, the angle. She got there. Down by a break is Bianca Andreescu serving love three. Calmly done. Lindy, where she's able to hit the ball from the back of the court. She's had to cope with that and Great way to get that break. evidences.title: [PDF] 2021 official rulebook - WTA Tour
evidences.content: B. RETIREMENTS If a player retires from a match, she is required to: a. Prior to retiring from the match, call for the PHCP and the Supervisor to provide the reason for the retirement; b. Immediately following the retirement, i. meet with the on-site PHCP and the Tournament Physician for an evaluation and assessment of the injury or illness and complete a WTA Medical Information Form prior to leaving the Tournament city; ii. submit a WTA Withdrawal Form to the on-site Supervisor; and iii. provide a statement containing the reason for her withdrawal and a suitable, in WTA Communications Department’s discretion, quotation that the WTA may release to the media and public. [...] APPENDIX J GLOSSARY 442 Walkover - Match did not begin because a) losing player was ill or injured or b) losing player was subjected to penalties of the Code of Conduct before first serve of match was struck or otherwise not permitted by the WTA or Tournament official to play. This would not be used when a Lucky Loser is substituted. Wild Card - Players chosen by the Tournament Director to fill designated spots in the draw. Withdrawal - The written communication by a player after her acceptance into a Tourna...

evidences.title: Renata Zarazúa upsets Madison Keys in 2025 US Open 1st round - ESPN
evidences.content: No. 12 Elina Svitolina was the highest seed after Keys to be eliminated on Day 2, falling to Anna Bondar 6-2, 6-4. Belgium's Elise Mertens, the No. 19 seed, beat American Alyssa Ahn 6-1, 6-0, and No. 17 seed Liudmila Samsonova got past Yuan Yue 2-6, 6-4, 6-4. Also advancing was No. 9 seed Elena Rybakina, a 6-3, 6-0 winner over 16-year-old American Julieta Pareja, the youngest player in either field at this year's US Open. Fellow American Taylor Townsend moved on in straight sets against Antonia Ruzic. No. 5 Mirra Andreeva crushed American Alycia Parks 6-0, 6-1 to also advance Monday night. Maria Sakkari defeated Tatjana Maria 6-3, 6-2 to secure her 50th major match win, the second most of any Greek player in the Open era after Stefanos Tsitsipas (64). [...] "In Mexico, yeah, it's probably not the most famous sport," Zarazúa said about tennis, which she picked up after starting out in gymnastics and diving. "I got into tennis," she explained, "just because my brother was playing, and he was like, 'I think this is a bit safer sport.'" In other women's singles results, two-time major champion Barbora Krejcikova took down 18-year-old Canadian Victoria Mboko, an emerging star seeded...
